working_on: Author product ADR 0097 + migration 0120 + rollback landing Tier-A Cluster 1 contestability substrate (per-edge confidence + provenance JSONB + counterexamples + version + review_status).
declared_scope: Land product/adr/0097 (Tier-A Cluster 1 — contestability substrate) carrying an ADR 0084 Load-bearing premises subsection enumerating 6 premises with falsifiers; pair an engine_memory decisions drawer per two-layer recording. Author paired migration product/seed-graph/migrations/0120_edges_contestability_substrate.sql adding 7 new columns (trace_confidence, llm_confidence, disagreement_flag, counterexamples JSONB, version, review_status, last_reviewed) + ALTERing existing 2 columns (confidence → expert_confidence rename; provenance TEXT → JSONB with backfill of 533 existing rows) with full contract header + Postcondition-Assertions block per ADR 0055 Layer 2.5; author paired rollback migration; apply via mcp__supabase__apply_migration and verify end-to-end (assertions pass, rollback round-trip stable). Cascade ADR 0094 + ADR 0095 amending T1-A premises to verified status. Defer graph_versions table + DISAGREEMENT_THRESHOLD value + unguarded-claim validator soft-warn implementation. phase: NA-tier-a-cluster-1
outcome_summary: Tier-A Cluster 1 contestability substrate landed end-to-end (drawer 14df75b5 for ADR 0097). Three commits: ADR 0097 + ADR 0094/0095 T1-A closure amendments + ROUTING.md S-0207 narrative + product/adr/README.md index entry (caddf21); migration 0120 + paired rollback (5735bbe); fixes (DROP DEFAULT pattern + Postcondition-Assertions corrected against verified production state + ADR 0097 Consequences amended for actual reviewer-token distribution; ce2be2e). Migration applied via mcp__supabase__apply_migration with end-to-end round-trip verification (apply → rollback → re-apply produced identical row-level state at each step). 533-row count invariant; 9 column-level changes (rename confidence → expert_confidence preserving 5-distinct REAL distribution from prior production-audit follow-ups; ALTER provenance TEXT → JSONB preserving 526 ai-seed + 7 qa_census_disposition_s_0183 reviewer tokens via USING jsonb_build_object; ADD COLUMN × 7 with appropriate defaults + CHECK constraints). Closes ADR 0094 T1-A (Tier-A implementability without re-decomposition — premise verified at this session via Cluster 1 landing in one migration with no scope re-shape) + ADR 0095 T1-A (Postgres+JSONB substrate-implementability — verified via plain typed columns + JSONB for two structured fields; no Apache AGE escalation). Both Consequences sections amended in-body with closure markers. 6 ADR 0084 load-bearing premises authored (T1-A contestability-as-atomic verified at synthesis convergence; T1-B disagreement_flag computation correctness + threshold value deferred to post-SQA calibration; T2-A/B/C adequacy premises deferred to first downstream consumer). engine_memory drawers paired (decisions 14df75b5; self-pushback drawer 1d47c76b — assertion-failure-vs-migration-correctness disambiguation when row count + schema shape correct but value distribution diverges from expected; lesson drawer fa216b3b — Postgres ALTER COLUMN TYPE with USING-clause backfill does NOT auto-cast DEFAULT expression, error 42804, requires DROP DEFAULT → ALTER TYPE → SET new DEFAULT pattern). Diary write 1489cc8c. Issues filed: #151 (priority:urgent — recurring Supabase pooler wedge during validate.py graph_audit, second occurrence within 24h post-S-0206, user-explicit 'won't work as a recurring issue') + #152 (deferred validate_edges_contestability_unguarded_high_confidence validator soft-warn implementation; gates on Cluster 2 retyping so the warn fires meaningfully on ~17 edges rather than all 533). Cold-review pass (SQL): 2 files (0120 + rollback), all match contract — Explore agent reported PASS on contract-vs-body match, discipline compliance, and round-trip safety. Next session work item: Cluster 2 (edge-type taxonomy expansion + three-relation layered separation) per ADR 0094 dependency order C1 → C2; claims migration slot 0130; retypes 516 pedagogical_prerequisite edges to soft_prerequisite default coordinating with this session's review_status: 'provisional' default. Carry-forward open questions Q1-F2 + Q1-F4 unchanged. Approved plan: ~/.claude/plans/clever-seeking-wigderson.md.

scope_delivery.explanation: Declared scope was 'land ADR 0097 + migration 0120 + paired rollback; apply + verify; cascade ADR 0094 + ADR 0095 T1-A; defer graph_versions table + DISAGREEMENT_THRESHOLD + unguarded-claim soft-warn'. All delivered. User-confirmed adjudications via plan-mode AskUserQuestion: confidence symmetric REAL (recommended) + ALTER+backfill (recommended) + graph_versions deferred (recommended) + threshold value deferred (recommended). Migration applied + round-trip verified end-to-end despite Supabase pooler wedge blocking pre-commit hooks twice during the session (user manually committed migration + fixes with --no-verify after Supabase project restart, per S-0207 wedge recovery flow tracked at Issue #151). Two additional Issues filed during the session beyond the deferrals named in scope: #151 for the recurring wedge itself; #152 formalizing the deferred unguarded-claim soft-warn as a follow-up Issue (gates on Cluster 2). Validate --final-check at shutdown clean (0 hard fails; 413 soft warns — 404 annotated baselines + 8 issue_collision + 1 engine_memory_zero_citations_after_search). ADR 0097 Consequences corrected mid-session when applied migration revealed actual production data was richer than the schema defaults the ADR initially described (526 ai-seed + 7 qa_census reviewer tokens, not 'human'; 5 distinct confidence values, not just 1.0) — Consequences amended in-body via fix-commit ce2be2e.

side_discoveries: Existing 533 edges did NOT carry the schema defaults the ADR initially assumed. Production data was richer: confidence had 5 distinct values (499×1.0 + 7×0.95 + 15×0.9 + 11×0.85 + 1×0.8 from prior production-audit follow-up sessions S-0123/S-0182/S-0183); provenance was 526 'ai-seed' (Phase 5 routine seed sessions supplied explicit reviewer rather than letting schema DEFAULT 'human' fire) + 7 'qa_census_disposition_s_0183' (S-0183 QA work). The migration's ALTER+backfill via USING preserved the actual data verbatim; the discrepancy was between ADR Consequences prose and reality, not between migration and data. Lesson: query distinct values BEFORE authoring Postcondition-Assertions for schema migrations against production data — schema DEFAULTs are a poor proxy.

Postgres ALTER COLUMN TYPE with USING-clause backfill does NOT auto-cast the DEFAULT expression — error 42804 'default for column X cannot be cast automatically to type Y' even when USING handles the data conversion. Required split into DROP DEFAULT → ALTER TYPE → SET new DEFAULT pattern for both forward (TEXT → JSONB) and rollback (JSONB → TEXT). Documented in lesson drawer fa216b3b; Cluster 2+ ADR authors should expect to hit the same pattern when doing column-type changes with DEFAULTs.

Issue #151 — Supabase pooler wedge recurred TWICE within 20 minutes of restart this session (vs. S-0206's single occurrence). The wedge structurally blocks the pre-commit hook's graph_audit phase via TCP poll on the AWS endpoint. User explicit on recurrence: 'this won't work as a recurring issue'. Mitigation candidates filed in Issue body (connection-cleanup hardening, connect/statement timeouts, monitoring instrumentation, pooler-mode investigation). Workaround for now: manual --no-verify commits after each Supabase restart, scoped to single commits — degrades build-session lifecycle materially.

Validate-history.jsonl now carries multiple ~1-hour entries from wedged validate runs that polluted the regression check (it fired false positives on duration_graph_audit_ms + duration_total_ms during this session). Worth a follow-up clean either via history pruning or via wedge-detection-and-exclusion in the regression check logic. Surface at next audit.

supabase_migrations.schema_migrations now carries three 0120 entries (forward + rollback + re-apply) — matches the S-0123 revert-as-separate-entry pattern, but means a future re-apply via apply_migration.py wrapper would hit exit 6 'already applied'. Not blocking for Cluster 2+ which uses higher prefixes.
